topics.topic_name: What do different food items contain?

cards.question: Food: It is the group of edible substances which provide energy to living beings and repair old tissues and build new tissues.
cards.text_html: Food: It is the group of edible substances which provide energy to living beings and repair old tissues and build new tissues.

cards.question: Nutrition: The process of consuming nutrients required for the growth and development of our body and to obtain energy is known as nutrition.
cards.text_html: Nutrition: The process of consuming nutrients required for the growth and development of our body and to obtain energy is known as nutrition.

cards.question: Our food contains three main substances called nutrients. These are fats, proteins and carbohydrates. In addition, our body requires water, salts (minerals), vitamins and fibres.
cards.text_html: Our food contains three main substances called nutrients. These are fats, proteins and carbohydrates. In addition, our body requires water, salts (minerals), vitamins and fibres.

topics.topic_name: What do various nutrients do for our body?

cards.question: Various food components are grouped into three classes: Energy-giving food: Carbohydrates and fats. Bodybuilding food: Proteins. Protective food: Vitamins and minerals.
cards.text_html:
Various food components are grouped into three classes:
  • Energy-giving food: Carbohydrates and fats.
  • Bodybuilding food: Proteins.
  • Protective food: Vitamins and minerals.

cards.question: Carbohydrates are also called energy-giving food. It is the main source of energy. Rice, wheat, banana, and potato are good sources of carbohydrates.
cards.text_html: Carbohydrates are also called energy-giving food. It is the main source of energy. Rice, wheat, banana, and potato are good sources of carbohydrates.

cards.question: There are three types of carbohydrates. Sugars : It is a simple carbohydrate with a sweet taste. Sources of sugar are glucose, Sugarcane, milk, and fruits; such as bananas, apples, grapes, etc. Starch: It is a complex carbohydrate. It is a tasteless, colorless white powder. Sources of starch are Wheat, maize, potato, and rice. Cellulose : It is present in plant cell wall. It is a complex carbohydrate. Humans cannot digest cellulose.
cards.text_html:
There are three types of carbohydrates.

  • Sugars: It is a simple carbohydrate with a sweet taste. Sources of sugar are glucose, Sugarcane, milk, and fruits; such as bananas, apples, grapes, etc.
  • Starch: It is a complex carbohydrate. It is a tasteless, colorless white powder. Sources of starch are Wheat, maize, potato, and rice.
  • Cellulose: It is present in plant cell wall. It is a complex carbohydrate. Humans cannot digest cellulose.

cards.question: Fats Fats give us more energy than carbohydrates. Food rich in fats is also called energy-giving foods. Fats are made up of carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen.
cards.text_html:
Fats
  • Fats give us more energy than carbohydrates.
  • Food rich in fats is also called energy-giving foods.
  • Fats are made up of carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen.

cards.question: Fats obtain from plants and animals like ghee, butter, oil, nuts etc.
cards.text_html: Fats obtain from plants and animals like ghee, butter, oil, nuts etc.

cards.question: An adult needs about 35 g fat every day. Fats are essential for the absorption of vitamins A, D, E and K in the body. Fat in our body also prevents heat loss from the body surface.
cards.text_html:
• An adult needs about 35 g fat every day.
  • Fats are essential for the absorption of vitamins A, D, E and K in the body.
  • Fat in our body also prevents heat loss from the body surface.

cards.question: Protein helps in body growth and repairs the tissues so it is also called body-building food. We get protein from milk, eggs, meat, fish and all kinds of pulse.
cards.text_html: Protein helps in body growth and repairs the tissues so it is also called body-building food. We get protein from milk, eggs, meat, fish and all kinds of pulse.

cards.question: The daily requirement of protein for adults is 1 gram per kilogram of body weight. When the body is building new tissue, more proteins are required, so growing children and pregnant ladies need more protein.
cards.text_html:
• The daily requirement of protein for adults is 1 gram per kilogram of body weight.
  • When the body is building new tissue, more proteins are required, so growing children and pregnant ladies need more protein.

cards.question: Vitamins Vitamins are complex organic compounds which are essential for the growth and maintenance of our body. Vitamins do not provide energy. Our body requires vitamins A, C, D, E, K, and B-complex.
cards.text_html:
Vitamins
  • Vitamins are complex organic compounds which are essential for the growth and maintenance of our body.
  • Vitamins do not provide energy.
  • Our body requires vitamins A, C, D, E, K, and B-complex.

cards.question: Fat-soluble vitamins: Vitamins A, D, E, and K are fat-soluble vitamins. Water-soluble Vitamins: Vitamins B and C are water-soluble vitamins.
cards.text_html:
Fat-soluble vitamins: Vitamins A, D, E, and K are fat-soluble vitamins.
Water-soluble Vitamins: Vitamins B and C are water-soluble vitamins.

cards.question: Sources of Vitamins: Vitamin A : Milk, fish, oil, papaya, radish Vitamin C: Orange, guava, tomato, lemon, amla Vitamin B: Liver, soyabean Vitamin D : Milk, Butter, egg, fish The function of vitamins: Vitamin A: keeps our eyes and skin healthy. Vitamin C: gives the body strength to fight against infections and diseases. Vitamin D: helps our body to use calcium for bones and teeth.
cards.text_html:
Sources of Vitamins:
Vitamin A: Milk, fish, oil, papaya, radish
Vitamin C: Orange, guava, tomato, lemon, amla
Vitamin B: Liver, soyabean
Vitamin D: Milk, Butter, egg, fish
The function of vitamins:
Vitamin A: keeps our eyes and skin healthy.
Vitamin C: gives the body strength to fight against infections and diseases.
Vitamin D: helps our body to use calcium for bones and teeth.

cards.question: Minerals: Iron, Iodine, calcium, phosphorus, sodium and potassium are common minerals. The sources of these are plants and animals.
cards.text_html: Minerals: Iron, Iodine, calcium, phosphorus, sodium and potassium are common minerals. The sources of these are plants and animals.

cards.question: Iron is needed for making blood and it makes our blood re d. It is found in apples and in green leafy vegetables.
cards.text_html: Iron is needed for making blood and it makes our blood red. It is found in apples and in green leafy vegetables.

cards.question: Sources of minerals: Iodine : Fish, spinach, curd, ginger Phosphorus: Milk, banana, soyabean, green chillies Iron: Liver, apple, spinach Calcium : Milk and egg
cards.text_html:
Sources of minerals:
Iodine: Fish, spinach, curd, ginger
Phosphorus: Milk, banana, soyabean, green chillies
Iron: Liver, apple, spinach
Calcium: Milk and egg

cards.question: Function of minerals: Iron: Essential for the formation of hemoglobin in the blood. Calcium : Essential for the formation and hardening of bones and teeth. Phosphorus: Formation of strong bones and teeth. Iodine: Essential for the formation of thyoxine hormone.
cards.text_html:
Function of minerals:
Iron: Essential for the formation of hemoglobin in the blood.
Calcium: Essential for the formation and hardening of bones and teeth.
Phosphorus: Formation of strong bones and teeth.
Iodine: Essential for the formation of thyoxine hormone.

cards.question: Water: The main source of water is fruits and vegetables. Water helps to transports food , waste, chemicals, and gasses throughout body.
cards.text_html:
Water: The main source of water is fruits and vegetables.
Water helps to transports food , waste, chemicals, and gasses throughout body.

cards.question: Water performs the following function in our body: It transports food material within the body. It helps in the formation of urine. It regulates our body temperature. It is an essential part of blood and digestive juices.
cards.text_html:
Water performs the following function in our body:

  1. It transports food material within the body.
  2. It helps in the formation of urine.
  3. It regulates our body temperature.
  4. It is an essential part of blood and digestive juices.

cards.question: Roughage: The components of food that cannot be digested by the body is called roughage. Roughage prevents constipation and ensures proper bowel movement. A high fibrous diet reduces heart disease and bowel Cancer.
cards.text_html: Roughage: The components of food that cannot be digested by the body is called roughage. Roughage prevents constipation and ensures proper bowel movement. A high fibrous diet reduces heart disease and bowel Cancer.

cards.question: Roughage performs the following function in our body: It is effective in regulating the process of digestion. It prevents constipation. It helps in the regular movement of bowels. It helps the food to pass down the alimentary canal properly.
cards.text_html:
Roughage performs the following function in our body:

  1. It is effective in regulating the process of digestion.
  2. It prevents constipation.
  3. It helps in the regular movement of bowels.
  4. It helps the food to pass down the alimentary canal properly.

cards.question: Sources of Roughage: Salad, vegetables and fruits, corn, apple.
cards.text_html:
Sources of Roughage:
Salad, vegetables and fruits, corn, apple.

cards.question: ​​​​​​​ Dietary fiber: The fibrous indigestible material present in any food is termed dietary fibre or roughage. It helps in preventing constipation. It adds bulk to the food and gives a sense of fullness after the meal. Salad, vegetables, sprouted grains, and fruits are the sources of roughages.
cards.text_html: ​​​​​​​Dietary fiber: The fibrous indigestible material present in any food is termed dietary fibre or roughage. It helps in preventing constipation. It adds bulk to the food and gives a sense of fullness after the meal. Salad, vegetables, sprouted grains, and fruits are the sources of roughages.

cards.question: ​​​​​​​ Test for starch: Take a small quantity of food items to be tested. Add 2 to 3 drops of dilute iodine solution to the food with a dropper. If a blue-black colour is produced, then starch is present in the given food.
cards.text_html:
​​​​​​​Test for starch: 

  1. Take a small quantity of food items to be tested.
  2. Add 2 to 3 drops of dilute iodine solution to the food with a dropper.
  3. If a blue-black colour is produced, then starch is present in the given food.

cards.question: Test for protein: Take 2 ml of the given food-stuff solution in a test tube. Add a little dilute sodium hydroxide solution till the mixture clears. Then add 2 to 3 drops of copper sulphate solution and shake well. If a violet colour appears in the solution, then protein is present in the given food stuff.
cards.text_html:
Test for protein:
  1. Take 2 ml of the given food-stuff solution in a test tube.
  2. Add a little dilute sodium hydroxide solution till the mixture clears.
  3. Then add 2 to 3 drops of copper sulphate solution and shake well.
  4. If a violet colour appears in the solution, then protein is present in the given food stuff.

cards.question: Test for fats: Take a small quantity of hard, solid food items and wrap them in filter paper. Crush the food item carefully by using a small hammer and rub the crushed food on the paper. Remove the crushed food from the paper, and hold the paper towards a source of light. The presence of oily patches on the paper shows the presence of fats in the given food item.
cards.text_html:
Test for fats: 
  1. Take a small quantity of hard, solid food items and wrap them in filter paper.
  2. Crush the food item carefully by using a small hammer and rub the crushed food on the paper.
  3. Remove the crushed food from the paper, and hold the paper towards a source of light.
  4. The presence of oily patches on the paper shows the presence of fats in the given food item.

cards.question: The diet which contains all the essential nutrients in the right proportion is called a balanced diet. The food we eat must have all the nutrients. It should provide the required energy.
cards.text_html: The diet which contains all the essential nutrients in the right proportion is called a balanced diet. The food we eat must have all the nutrients. It should provide the required energy.

cards.question: Protein group: Some of the food items such as meat, eggs, fish, beans, nuts, peas, etc.
cards.text_html: Protein group: Some of the food items such as meat, eggs, fish, beans, nuts, peas, etc.

cards.question: Milk group: Some of the food items such as milk, curd, cheese, etc.
cards.text_html: Milk group: Some of the food items such as milk, curd, cheese, etc.

cards.question: Vegetable and fruit group: Fresh vegetables and fruits.
cards.text_html: Vegetable and fruit group: Fresh vegetables and fruits.

cards.question: Cereal group: Some of the food items such as rice, bread, chapatti, noodles, etc.
cards.text_html: Cereal group: Some of the food items such as rice, bread, chapatti, noodles, etc.

cards.question: A balanced diet should have the following three qualities: It should be rich in various essential nutrients like minerals and vitamins. It should provide just enough raw material needed for the growth, development, repair and replacement of tissues of the body. It should provide the right quantum of energy required by the body.
cards.text_html:
A balanced diet should have the following three qualities:

  1. It should be rich in various essential nutrients like minerals and vitamins.
  2. It should provide just enough raw material needed for the growth, development, repair and replacement of tissues of the body.
  3. It should provide the right quantum of energy required by the body.

cards.question: A deficiency of one or more nutrients can cause diseases or disorders in our body.
cards.text_html: A deficiency of one or more nutrients can cause diseases or disorders in our body.

cards.question: What are deficiency diseases?
cards.answer: Diseases that occur due to lack of nutrients over a long period are called deficiency diseases.
cards.text_html: What are deficiency diseases?
cards.answer_html: Diseases that occur due to lack of nutrients over a long period are called deficiency diseases.

cards.question: All deficiency diseases can be prevented by taking a ? .
cards.answer: All deficiency diseases can be prevented by taking a balanced diet .
cards.text_html: All deficiency diseases can be prevented by taking a ?.
cards.answer_html: All deficiency diseases can be prevented by taking a balanced diet.

cards.question: Obesity: Excessive intake of carbohydrates causes overweight it is called Obesity.
cards.text_html: Obesity: Excessive intake of carbohydrates causes overweight it is called Obesity.

cards.question: Kwashiorkor: A disease caused by a lack of proteins in the body. The stomach becomes swollen and the legs became thin.
cards.text_html: Kwashiorkor: A disease caused by a lack of proteins in the body. The stomach becomes swollen and the legs became thin.

cards.question: Symptoms of Kwashiorkor Stunted growth Skin becomes dry and scaly The body becomes reddish. The body swells up due to retention water, and a large pot-like belly. Limbs become thin and bony.
cards.text_html:
Symptoms of Kwashiorkor
  1. Stunted growth
  2. Skin becomes dry and scaly
  3. The body becomes reddish.
  4. The body swells up due to retention water, and a large pot-like belly.
  5. Limbs become thin and bony.

cards.question: Marasmus : A disease caused by the lack of protein and carbohydrates. The child becomes so thin that loose folds of skin can be seen all over the body.
cards.text_html: Marasmus: A disease caused by the lack of protein and carbohydrates. The child becomes so thin that loose folds of skin can be seen all over the body.

cards.question: Symptoms of Marasmus : The body becomes very lean and thin and so weak that the child may not be able to move. Skin becomes dry, thin, and wrinkled. Ribs become very prominent. Sunken eyes. Unable to digest and absorb food and may suffer from diarrhea.
cards.text_html:
Symptoms  of Marasmus:
  1. The body becomes very lean and thin and so weak that the child may not be able to move.
  2. Skin becomes dry, thin, and wrinkled.
  3. Ribs become very prominent.
  4. Sunken eyes.
  5. Unable to digest and absorb food and may suffer from diarrhea.

cards.question: Goiter : A disease caused by lack of mineral iodine. In these diseases, the thyroid gland becomes swollen and reduces the production of thyroxin that controls growth. The main symptom is that the neck of the person appears to be swollen.
cards.text_html: Goiter: A disease caused by lack of mineral iodine. In these diseases, the thyroid gland becomes swollen and reduces the production of thyroxin that controls growth. The main symptom is that the neck of the person appears to be swollen.

cards.question: Rickets: In this bones becomes soft and get bent and deformed. It is caused by a lack of vitamins D. Lack of phosphorus also affects bones and teeth.
cards.text_html: Rickets: In this bones becomes soft and get bent and deformed. It is caused by a lack of vitamins D. Lack of phosphorus also affects bones and teeth.

cards.question: Scurvy: It is caused due to the deficiency of vitamin C. ​​​​​​​Symptoms of scurvy include: Bleeding from gums Loosening of teeth Wounds do not heal easily.
cards.text_html:
Scurvy: It is caused due to the deficiency of vitamin C.
​​​​​​​Symptoms of scurvy include:
  1. Bleeding from gums
  2. Loosening of teeth
  3. Wounds do not heal easily.

cards.question: Night blindness: It is caused due to the deficiency of vitamin A. Severe deficiency of vitamin A may lead to complete blindness. In this disease, a person is not able to see in dim light.
cards.text_html: Night blindness: It is caused due to the deficiency of vitamin A. Severe deficiency of vitamin A may lead to complete blindness. In this disease, a person is not able to see in dim light.

cards.question: Beri-beri: It is caused due to the deficiency of vitamin B 1 . The main symptoms are: The muscles of the person become very weak. The person has little energy to do work. The person may suffer from heart failure and become paralysed.
cards.text_html:
Beri-beri: It is caused due to the deficiency of vitamin B1.
The main symptoms are:
  1. The muscles of the person become very weak.
  2. The person has little energy to do work.
  3. The person may suffer from heart failure and become paralysed.

cards.question: Bone and tooth decay: The deficiency of calcium in the diet of children leads to brittle bones and tooth decay.
cards.text_html: Bone and tooth decay: The deficiency of calcium in the diet of children leads to brittle bones and tooth decay.

cards.question: Anaemia: It is caused by the deficiency of iron. The main symptoms are: The person looks pale and tired. The nails turn white. The person loses weight.
cards.text_html:
Anaemia: It is caused by the deficiency of iron. The main symptoms are: 
  1. The person looks pale and tired.
  2. The nails turn white.
  3. The person loses weight.

cards.question: Vitmains/Minerals Deficiency Diseases Symptoms Vitamin A Night blindness Poor vision, loss of vision in darkness Vitamin B1 Beriberi Weak muscles, fatigue Vitamin C Scurvy Bleeding gums Vitamin D Rickets Bent bones Calcium Osteomalacia Weak bones, tooth decay Iodine Goitre Swelling in neck Iron Anemia General weakness, fatigue
cards.text_html:
Vitmains/Minerals Deficiency Diseases Symptoms
Vitamin A Night blindness Poor vision, loss of vision in darkness
Vitamin B1 Beriberi Weak muscles, fatigue
Vitamin C Scurvy Bleeding gums
Vitamin D Rickets Bent bones
Calcium Osteomalacia Weak bones, tooth decay
Iodine Goitre Swelling in neck
Iron Anemia General weakness, fatigue
